IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: THE ORGANIZATION PROVIDES PRIMARY MEDICAL, BEHAVIOR HEALTH, AND DENTAL CARE TO THE POPULATION OF SAN JOAQUIN, AND SOLANO COUNTIES WITHOUT REGARD TO PATIENTS' ABILITY TO PAY.
IRS990.MissionDesc: WORKING TOGETHER TO IMPROVE HEALTH AND WELL-BEING IN OUR COMMUNITIES.
IRS990.Desc: COMMUNITY MEDICAL CENTERS IS ONE OF THE LARGEST PROVIDERS OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES IN CENTRAL CALIFORNIA. WE OPERATE 14 FULLY LICENSED AND 21 INTERMITTENT CLINICS IN 2 CALIFORNIA COUNTIES. OUR CLINICIANS COMPLETE OVER 462,000 PATIENT ENCOUNTERS ANNUALLY, AND THE NUMBER INCREASES EACH YEAR. WE HAVE FULLY IMPLEMENTED AN INTEGRATED ELECTRONIC HEALTH RECORDS (EHR) SYSTEM IN ALL PRACTICES AT ALL SITES. WE ARE ACCREDITED BY THE JOINT COMMISSION ON ACCREDITATION OF HEALTHCARE ORGANIZATIONS, AND WE STRIVE TO BE THE PATIENT-CENTERED MEDICAL HOME FOR ALL FAMILY MEMBERS. WE OFFER AN EXTENSIVE ARRAY OF SERVICES TO ALL, REGARDLESS OF THEIR CIRCUMSTANCES. WE ALSO CONTINUE TO ADD NEW MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, RECRUIT OUTSTANDING PHYSICIANS IN NEW SPECIALTIES.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART II, LINE 10, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: MISCELLANEOUS INCOME - 2019 AMOUNT: $ 746. 2020 AMOUNT: $ 0. 2021 AMOUNT: $ 0. 2022 AMOUNT: $ 0. 2023 AMOUNT: $ 1,431,212.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION HAS BEEN RECOGNIZED AS EXEMPT FROM INCOME TAXES UNDER SECTION 501 OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND A SIMILAR PROVISION OF STATE LAW. HOWEVER, THE ORGANIZATION IS SUBJECT TO FEDERAL INCOME TAX ON ANY UNRELATED BUSINESS TAXABLE INCOME. AS OF JUNE 30, 2024 AND 2023, THE ORGANIZATION HAD NO UNRECOGNIZED TAX POSITIONS OR UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS REQUIRING ACCRUAL. THEREFORE, NO PROVISION FOR INCOME TAXES HAS BEEN PROVIDED IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.
A PORTION OF THE EXECUTIVE TEAM'S ANNUAL BONUS IS CONTINGENT ON PROFIT MARGIN.

NON-FIXED PAYMENTS: COMMUNITY MEDICAL CENTERS INC. PAID INCENTIVE AWARDS TO PROVIDERS. PROVIDERS ARE ELIGIBLE TO RECEIVE TWO TYPES OF INCENTIVE AWARDS. THE FIRST IS QUALITY INCENTIVE, WHICH IS PAID FOR REACHING GOALS ESTABLISHED BY THE ORGANIZATION. THE SECOND INCENTIVE IS FOR PRODUCTIVITY, BASED ON BILLED ENCOUNTERS. THE SPECIFIC EXPECTATION IS SET BY PRACTICE SPECIALTY, ADJUSTED FOR EXPECTED SCHEDULE TIME, AND FOR TIME ALLOCATED FOR ADMINISTRATIVE DUTIES AND COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT ON BEHALF OF THE ORGANIZATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FORM 990 IS PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT ACCOUNTING FIRM AND REVIEWED INTERNALLY BY THE CEO AND CFO BEFORE FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION'S LEGAL COUNSEL HAS PROVIDED ASSISTANCE TO THE BOARD TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST AND THE ORGANIZATION'S BY LAWS. WHEN CONFLICT OF INTEREST ISSUES ARISE, THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS WITH THE DIRECTOR OF THEIR LEGAL COUNSEL ADDRESSES THE ISSUES ACCORDINGLY. THE BOARD HAS BEEN MADE AWARE OF THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST REQUIREMENTS BY THEIR LEGAL COUNSEL. NO DIRECTOR, OFFICER, EMPLOYEE OR AGENT SHALL PARTICIPATE IN THE SELECTION, AWARD OR ADMINISTRATION OF ANY CONTRACT OR OTHER AFFILIATION RELATING TO OPERATIONS CONDUCTED BY THE CORPORATION OR FOR THE FURNISHING OF SERVICES OR SUPPLIES TO THE CORPORATION, IN WHICH HE OR SHE OR HIS/HER IMMEDIATE FAMILY OR BUSINESS PARTNER HAS A REAL OR POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST OR INVOLVING AN ORGANIZATION WITH WHOM HE OR SHE IS NEGOTIATING OR HAS ANY ARRANGEMENT CONCERNING EMPLOYMENT. IF A CONTRACT OR AFFILIATION EXISTS, IT MAY BE APPROVED OR AUTHORIZED BY A MAJORITY OF THE DISINTERESTED DIRECTORS. IF THE FACTS OF ANY SUCH INTEREST BY A DIRECTOR SHALL HAVE BEEN DISCLOSED TO THE BOARD BY THE INTERESTED DIRECTOR SUCH INTERESTED DIRECTOR ABSTAINS FROM VOTING IN THAT REGARD.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: INDUSTRY COMPENSATION SURVEYS FOR LIKE ORGANIZATIONS IN SIMILAR GEOGRAPHIC AREAS ARE REVIEWED BY THE BOARD AND THE CEO TO DETERMINE KEY EMPLOYEE COMPENSATION. THE DELIBERATION AND FINAL DECISIONS ARE CONTEMPORANEOUSLY DOCUMENTED AS PART OF THE PROCESS. THE LAST REVIEW WAS DONE IN 2023. THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS ESTABLISHED THE CEO'S SALARY. SIMILAR METHODOLOGIES WERE USED TO DETERMINE THE COMPENSATION OF OTHER OFFICERS AND KEY EMPLOYEES. TWO REVIEWS WERE DONE. ONE WAS COMPLETED BY THE HUMAN RESOURCES DEPARTMENT AND ONE WAS CONTRACTED BY THE BOARD TO BE DONE BY A HEALTH CARE CONSULTING AGENCY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON WRITTEN REQUEST TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES.
